title: anthemis tinctoria subsp. fussii
intro: Anthemis tinctoria subsp. fussii, also known as the woolly chamomile or dyer's chamomile, is a subspecies of the chamomile plant native to Central Europe. The medicinal properties of Anthemis tinctoria subsp. fussii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, digestive issues, and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to calm the nervous system, promote relaxation, and aid in the treatment of insomnia and anxiety disorders. Herbal preparations of Anthemis tinctoria subsp. fussii include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
